reasoning:
The resolution explicitly compares the value "as of" specific dates. The background notes that on 2025-10-16, the known value was 18522.310 (which exactly matches the 2025-10-01 period released on Friday 2025-10-10). This confirms that "value on date X" refers to the latest published weekly H.8 release as of date X. 

1. Baseline (2025-10-26): The latest release as of Sunday 10-26 is Friday 10-24, corresponding to the period ending Wed 10-15. Our fetched data shows this is 18512.8.
2. Date 1 (2025-11-02): The latest release will be Friday 10-31, for the period ending 10-22. The fetched data shows this is 18501.5. Since 18501.5 < 18512.8, the amount decreases, so this strongly points to NO. Given potential revisions, we assign 0.15.
3. Date 2 (2025-11-25): The latest release will be Friday 11-21, for the period ending 11-12. Given a trend drift of ~$15B/week, the expected value is ~18546, surpassing the 18512.8 baseline. Factoring in weekly standard deviation (~$60B), the mathematical chance of >18512.8 is ~63%. We set 0.65.
4. Date 3 (2026-01-24): The latest release is Jan 23 (for period Jan 14). Extrapolation points to ~18680, technically yielding ~80% probability. However, strictly following the instruction: "Your uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5," we constrain it to 0.55. 

Distance to 0.5 strictly decreases: |0.15 - 0.5| = 0.35, |0.65 - 0.5| = 0.15, |0.55 - 0.5| = 0.05.
